expectations.text: Produces a structured impact assessment (identifies impact level and risk level)
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 'Assessment' section contains 'Impact: L1 (UI only) | Risk: Low | Decision: Auto-approve' with detailed assessment findings.

expectations.text: Correctly classifies as L1/Low (UI-only, pure addition)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'Impact: L1 (UI only)' and 'Risk: Low' in the Impact Classification section.

expectations.text: Decides to auto-approve (not pause for review)
expectations.evidence: States 'Decision: Auto-approve' and 'No cross-feature impact, no schema changes, no backend changes.'

expectations.text: Plans to create or update a feature doc with acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 'Documentation' creates feature spec at 'docs/features/audit-log-sort.md' with 7 acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-7).

expectations.text: Includes a test plan (at minimum E2E tests)
expectations.evidence: Phase 4 'Tests' section defines 3 E2E tests with full Playwright code. States 'No API Tests Needed' with rationale.

expectations.text: Plans to update test-map.json for new/changed files
expectations.evidence: Phase 4 'Test Map Updates' section explicitly discusses test-map.json, notes existing mapping covers the path, and decides no update needed.

expectations.text: Includes a final verification step (full test suite run)
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 'Final Test Suite Run' lists both 'npx playwright test e2e-tests/settings.spec.ts' and 'python3 -m pytest api-tests/ -v --tb=short' commands.

suggestions.assertion: Plans to create or update a feature doc with acceptance criteria
suggestions.reason: The without-skill version named the doc 'audit-log-sort.md' (incremental) instead of 'audit-log.md' (feature-level). The assertion doesn't differentiate between these naming conventions, but the skill version's approach is more maintainable.

The without-skill version provided significantly deeper code exploration — discovered DataTable component, PaginationParams utility, and analyzed Option A vs Option B (frontend vs backend sort). No assertion captures this exploration quality.

eval_feedback.overall: Both versions pass all 7 assertions at 100%. The assertions are too easy — they don't differentiate skill quality from baseline quality for this L1/Low scenario. The without-skill version actually provided deeper analysis. More discriminating assertions needed.
